fields.description:
## What
Make the `rebar metrics` surface (this epic's registry + `rebar metrics` command) **usable by the tools that do code-health work, and discoverable in project docs** â€” so the metrics are not a dead-end artifact. Two deliverables: (1) teach the **rebar-janitor** skill to consult `rebar metrics` as durable, historical code-health evidence during Discovery; (2) reference the metrics surface from the project's canonical documentation so any agent/contributor can find it.

## Why
The janitor's Discovery phase already quantifies "size & growth" and "temporal decay" with one-shot tools (`cloc`/`tokei`/`ast-grep`, `git log`). `rebar metrics` adds the **trend** dimension those miss â€” the code-health-lens metrics the registry actually ships â€” module-size distribution and oversized-module count against the locked 800-line cap, churn, and the refactor-to-addition ratio â€” plus snapshot-backed complexity/clone/coverage trends where those snapshots have accrued (otherwise `unavailable`) â€” as a single durable source. A janitor that cites `rebar metrics` values as evidence produces findings grounded in measured decay over time, not just a single snapshot. And metrics no agent knows to read provide no value, so discoverability (docs) is part of "done".

## Files
- `examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md` â€” in the "Size & growth" / temporal-decay guidance, add that a subagent should run `rebar metrics --since <date> --until <date> --output json` (when the repo is a rebar store) and cite the registered code-health-lens metric values (module-size distribution and oversized-module count vs the 800 cap, churn, refactor-to-addition ratio; complexity/clone/coverage are snapshot-backed and may read `unavailable` until backfilled) as evidence â€” as the historical/trend complement to the one-shot `cloc`/`tokei`/`ast-grep` pass, not a replacement. Note the `unavailable` state (a metric whose signal has not accrued yet) so the agent does not treat absence as zero.
- `docs/user-guide.md` â€” add a short, hand-written **"Metrics"** section: what `rebar metrics --since/--until [--output json]` surfaces (the four lenses â€” `agent_process`, `code_health`, `delivery`, `gate_economics`), the `source`/`confidence` labels and the `unavailable{reason,accruing_since}` state, and that backfilled/classified values are segregated from authoritative structural series. This is the human-discoverable home (the CLI-reference is auto-generated syntax; this is the "what/why/when").
- `docs/reuse-surface.md` â€” reference the reusable `rebar.metrics` subsystem (the `MetricSpec`/`REGISTRY` declarative registry + `is_authoritative` segregation helper) so library consumers discover it.
- `AGENTS.md` â€” add ONE line to the "Where to read" pointer list: **Metrics** â†’ `docs/user-guide.md` (command + lenses) and `docs/reuse-surface.md` (the registry), keeping the file lean per its own convention.

## Approach
Documentation- and skill-only; no `src/rebar` change and no new behavior â€” it makes the already-shipped `rebar metrics` command and registry usable and findable. It stacks on the metrics command (9a5a) so it references the command's real, landed interface (and so `docs/cli-reference.md` already lists the subcommand). Additive and reversible (revert = remove the references).

## Tests
- Doc/skill assertions (no runtime code): a check that `examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md` mentions `rebar metrics`; that `docs/user-guide.md` has a Metrics section naming the four lens values (agent_process/code_health/delivery/gate_economics) and the `unavailable` state; that `AGENTS.md`'s "Where to read" list has a metrics pointer; that `docs/reuse-surface.md` references `rebar.metrics`. (These can be a lightweight `tests/scripts/`-tier grep test or folded into the existing docs-index/README drift checks.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md` instructs a Discovery subagent to consult `rebar metrics --since <date> --until <date> --output json` for the code-health lens and cite its values as evidence â€” verified by a grep/test asserting the `rebar metrics` reference and the `unavailable`-state caveat are present.
- [ ] `docs/user-guide.md` contains a "Metrics" section documenting the `rebar metrics` command, the four lenses (`agent_process` / `code_health` / `delivery` / `gate_economics`), and the `source`+`confidence` labels + `unavailable` state â€” asserted by a grep/test for the section heading and the lens names.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md`'s "Where to read" list contains a metrics pointer into `docs/` â€” asserted by a grep/test.
- [ ] `docs/reuse-surface.md` references the `rebar.metrics` registry subsystem â€” asserted by a grep/test.
- [ ] the docs-index / README drift gates still pass (no stale generated docs) after the edits.
